lede: A look at January 30 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Prince Hashem bin Abdullah and Melissa Duncan, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: World Health Organization
events.desc: The World Health Organization declares the COVID-19 pandemic to be a Public Health Emergency of International Concern.

events.title: Kenya Airways Flight 431
events.desc: Kenya Airways Flight 431 crashes into the Atlantic Ocean off the coast of Ivory Coast, killing 169.

events.title: The Troubles
events.desc: The Troubles: Bloody Sunday: British paratroopers open fire on anti-internment marchers in Derry, Northern Ireland, killing 13 people; another person later dies of injuries sustained.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: Tet Offensive launch by forces of the Viet Cong and North Vietnamese Army against South Vietnam, the United States, and their allies.

events.title: Nguyễn Khánh
events.desc: In a bloodless coup, General Nguyễn Khánh overthrows General Dương Văn Minh's military junta in South Vietnam.

events.title: MS Hans Hedtoft
events.desc: MS Hans Hedtoft, specifically designed to operate in icebound seas, strikes an iceberg on her maiden voyage and sinks, killing all 95 aboard.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Japanese forces invade the island of Ambon in the Dutch East Indies. Some 300 captured Allied troops are killed after the surrender. One-quarter of the remaining POWs remain alive at the end of the war.

events.title: 30 January 1939 Reichstag speech
events.desc: During a speech in the Reichstag, Adolf Hitler makes a prediction about the end of the Jewish race in Europe if another world war were to occur.

events.title: Mahatma Gandhi
events.desc: Indian pacifist and leader Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi is released from prison by Jan C. Smuts after being tried and sentenced to two months in jail earlier in the month.

events.title: Richard Lawrence (failed assassin)
events.desc: In the first assassination attempt against a President of the United States, Richard Lawrence attempts to shoot president Andrew Jackson, but fails and is subdued by a crowd, including several congressmen as well as Jackson himself.

births.name: Prince Hashem bin Abdullah
births.line: second son of King Abdullah II of Jordan

births.name: Peter Agre
births.line: American physician and biologist, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Catherine O'Hara, Canadian-American actress, comedian and screenwriter
Mahatma Gandhi, leader of the Indian independence movement against British rule
Charles I of England, Scotland, and Ireland

observances.text: Christian Feast Day: Anthony the Great (Coptic Church)
Christian Feast Day: Charles, King and Martyr (various provinces of the Anglican Communion)
